data.title: AdvanSix beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• AdvanSix press release (ASIX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $359.94M (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $19.09M.
  • 4Q25 Cash Flow from Operations of $64 million

    2025 planned plant turnarounds completed successfully at lower end of range.

  • Driving improved cash flow in 2026 through fixed cost savings, turnaround scope, risk-based prioritization of capital expenditures, and tax benefits.

  • Outlook: 

  • Expect Capital Expenditures of $75 to $95 million in 2026 versus approximately $116 million in 2025, reflecting a risk-based prioritization of base investments and enterprise programs with continued progression of growth programs including SUSTAIN
  • Expect pre-tax income impact of plant turnarounds to be $20 to $25 million in 2026 versus approximately $25 million in 2025
  • Expect cash flow benefit in 2026 and beyond from 45Q carbon capture tax credits and 100% bonus depreciation

data.title: Hudbay Minerals introduces new quarterly dividend of CAD 0.01
data.description:
• Hudbay Minerals (HBM) declares CAD 0.01/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 0.12%
  • Payable March 27; for shareholders of record March 10; ex-div March 10.
  • Hudbay’s Board of Directors has approved a new quarterly dividend of C$0.01 per share due to better-than-expected financial performance. The total annual dividend is now C$0.04 per share, marking a 100% increase from the previous amount annual dividend, which was paid semi-annually, representing the first dividend increase in the Company’s history.
  • See HBM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Sumitomo Pharma shares slide nearly 16% despite panel backing for iPS-derived parkinson’s treatment
data.description:
Sumitomo Pharma (SMDPY) shares fell on Friday, a day after a Japanese government panel backed conditional approval of its regenerative therapy based on technology developed by a Nobel Prize–winning scientist.

According to Japan’s Ministry of Health, Labor, and Welfare, formal approval will be granted within the next one to two months, NHK reported.

The stock fell as much as 15.6% on Friday on what appeared to be profit-taking after the company was granted a seven-year clearance. Sumitomo Pharma shares, up more than 300% in 2025, reached their highest level since 2019 last week as investor optimism grew around its Parkinson’s treatment.

“Reports of the smooth green-lighting of the drugs could lead to some profit-taking in the short term,” Morgan Stanley MUFG analysts, including Shinichiro Muraoka, wrote in a report.

The endorsement comes years after Kyoto University professor Shinya Yamanaka, who later won a Nobel Prize for his stem cell research, first succeeded in generating iPS cells from mice in 2006.

data.title: Nvidia reiterating AI chip demand, OpenAI's funding among Wedbush's top 10 things to stop tech slide
data.description:
Wedbush (IVES) highlighted its top 10 things that will stop the tech slide and the AI "Ghost Trade" overhang.

"Today the 'AI is a threat to every industry narrative...with software front and center as enemy #1' trade in this market. The AI trade has been the 'fear of the unknown' for the tech and software sector in particular. For the bulls, it's like fighting a ghost, while the cap-ex dollars approach ~$700 billion this year alone...the fear is AI startups will be the Nightmare on Elm Street horror show for the tech industry," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

The analysts added that the AI tech trade is at a tenuous crossroads, and it is time for tech leaders to lead.

The analysts noted their top 10 tech headlines to restart tech AI trade higher:

1. OpenAI (OPENAI) raises their $100B round and puts the loud noise to rest.

2. Nvidia's (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang reiterates the massive AI chip demand trajectory on next week's conference call and surpasses Street whisper numbers.

Nvidia is slated to report its fiscal fourth quarter results on Feb. 25. 

3. Oracle has strong initial success in its key $45B to $50B capital raise.

4. Salesforce (CRM) delivers with earnings/guidance showing AI monetization has started.

5. Software mergers and acquisitions, or M&A, begin over the next few months with strategic, larger public deals.

6. Apple (AAPL) finally releases its first phase of Siri AI and starts the consumer AI chapter.

7. CrowdStrike (CRWD) is the first cybersecurity player starting to truly monetize on AI and that shows up in its next earnings report.

8. AI monetization starts to show up in March results from Microsoft (MSFT) and ServiceNow (NOW).

9. Meta Platforms (META) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google digital advertising AI monetization ramps up in the first half of 2026.

10. Enterprises start to implement Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude and have scaling and security issues.

data.title: AngloGold Ashanti reports mixed Q4 results; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• AngloGold Ashanti press release (AU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.90 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $3.03B (+76.2% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Outlook: Gold production for the Group is forecast to range between 2.80Moz and 3.17Moz in 2026.

    Total cash cost per ounce* for the Group is forecast to range between $1,315/oz and $1,430/oz. The midpoint of these range represents an approximate 11% increase (or $130/oz) compared to 2025, with approximately 50% of the increase reflecting higher royalty costs and 50% of the increase reflecting cost inflation. AISC per ounce* for the Group is forecast to range between $1,780/oz and $1,990/oz in 2026.

data.title: Markets AM Need to Know: cheaper Cybertruck, Miran little less dovish, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as investors awaited key economic data, including gross domestic product and personal consumption expenditures figures due later in the session.

Now, here are five news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

Tesla introduces lower-priced Cybertruck: Tesla (TSLA) on Thursday unveiled a new, more affordable U.S. Cybertruck at $59,990, making the dual-motor all-wheel-drive model the company’s lowest-priced version of the electric pickup to date. The automaker also slashed the price of its top-tier Cyberbeast model to $99,990 from $114,990, according to pricing information on its website, as the company moves to broaden the truck’s appeal to a wider customer base.

Nvidia nears $30 billion OpenAI stake: U.S. chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) is close to finalizing a $30 billion investment into OpenAI that will replace the long-term $100 billion commitment agreed upon by the companies last year, the Financial Times reported Thursday. The equity investment is part of a funding round on track to raise more than $100 billion and will value OpenAI at $730 billion, not including the new capital.

Oppenheimer projects sales upside for Nvidia: Investment firm Oppenheimer expects Nvidia (NVDA) to deliver a “typical” upside of between $2 billion and $3 billion when it reports fourth-quarter results. Analyst Rick Schafer wrote in a note to clients that he sees potential for the chipmaker to beat Street estimates of $65.6 billion for Q4 and $71.6 billion for Q1, driven by demand for the GB300 Ultra chip.

NASA releases critical Starliner report: NASA’s new administrator, Jared Isaacman, blasted Boeing (BA) and the space agency Thursday for their handling of the botched Starliner spacecraft mission that left two astronauts stranded on the International Space Station for months. The agency released a 300-page report detailing numerous technical problems, poor communication, and management failures before and during the 2024 crewed flight, with Isaacman blaming NASA managers for failing to intervene and return the astronauts home more quickly.

Fed’s Miran signals slower rate cuts: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said in an interview published on Substack that he is leaning toward less aggressive cuts than he was two months ago, citing an economy that has evolved differently than expected. Miran, who recently resigned from the White House’s Council of Economic Advisers, in December projected rate cuts to bring the federal funds rate target range to slightly above 2%, representing a 50-basis-point increase from his September view.

data.title: Lamar Advertising reports mixed Q4 results; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Lamar Advertising press release (LAMR): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.50 misses by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $595.9M (+2.8% Y/Y) beats by $3.2M.
  • Diluted AFFO per share increased 1.4% to $2.24 for the three months ended December 31, 2025 as compared to $2.21 for the same period in 2024.
  • Acquisition-adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2025 increased 2.1% as compared to acquisition-adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $288.9 million versus $278.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, an increase of 3.7%.

  • Guidance

    We expect net income per diluted share for fiscal year 2026 to be between $5.72 and $5.83, with diluted AFFO per share between $8.50 and $8.70  (vs. $9.52 consensus). See “Supplemental Schedules Unaudited REIT Measures and Reconciliations to GAAP Measures” for reconciliation to GAAP.

data.title: GRAIL GAAP EPS of -$2.44 beats by $0.22, revenue of $43.6M beats by $122.0K
data.description:
• GRAIL, Inc. press release (GRAL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$2.44 beats by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $43.6M (+14.0% Y/Y) beats by $122.0K.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was $(71.8) million.
  • U.S. Galleri revenue grew 31% year over year to $41.3 million.

data.title: Hedge fund managers give mixed signals on Nvidia
data.description:
Wall Street hedge funds and portfolio managers delivered mixed signals on Nvidia (NVDA) in their Q4 filings; however, it is important to note that many managers remain heavily invested in the chipmaker.

George Soros-led Soros Fund Management added 22% to its Nvidia (NVDA) position in Q4 to 665K shares worth $124M. 

Point 72 Asset Management increased holdings in the chipmaker (NVDA) by 38% to 9.99M, valued at $1.86B, at the number one spot. Third Point added 4% to its Nvidia (NVDA) stake, totaling 2.95M, worth $550M, still holding the number two spot on the list.

Morgan Stanley had no major take on the stock; the investment bank continues holding Nvidia (NVDA) at number two in top holdings with over 323M shares valued at $60.68B. Similarly, Corvex Management had a flat investment in the company.

While Cathie Wood's ARK Investment Management boosted holdings in the semiconductor company (NVDA) by 8% to over 1.25M shares worth around $234M.

Wall Street major Goldman Sachs (GS) dropped holdings marginally by 1% to $31B, still at the top of the list. JP Morgan (JPM) dropped 7% of holdings in Q4 to 456M shares, worth $85B, though Nvidia (NVDA) stood with the topmost weightage.

Tiger Global dropped its stake by 6%, while Alex Sacerdote's Whale Rock chopped a whopping 62% in the semiconductor company (NVDA) to 1.2M shares worth $224M.

SoftBank (SFTBY) (SFTBF) completely liquidated its over 19.22M shares of the semiconductor giant (NVDA) as founder Masayoshi Son decided it would help finance further investments in AI.

Ray Dalio's Bridgewater reduced holdings by 12% to over 132K shares, valued at $24.35M. Coatue also dropped a 7% stake.

Renaissance Technologies slashed an 84% Nvidia (NVDA) stake to 871K shares valued at 162M, while Appaloosa cut by 11% to 1.7M shares.

Check out SA's quant rating on Nvidia (NVDA): Hold 3.49.

ETFs with high weightage in Nvidia (NVDA) stock: (SHOC), (SMH), (SMHX), (MAGS), (ESPO), (DRIV), (VGT), (SOXX), (SEMI), and (SFY).

data.title: Amazon's AWS says user error, not AI error, caused outage in December
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) Amazon Web Services noted that an outage in December 2025 was due to user error, not AI error, denying a Financial Times report that the errors involved its own AI tools.

An AWS spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that the media reports are not accurate, and the claim that these events were caused by AI is false. The spokesperson noted that in these instances, the root cause was user error, not AI error.

"This brief event was the result of user error-specifically misconfigured access controls-not AI. The service interruption was an extremely limited event last year when a single service (AWS Cost Explorer-which helps customers visualize, understand, and manage AWS costs and usage over time) in one of our two Regions in Mainland China was affected. This event didn't impact compute, storage, database, AI technologies, or any other of the hundreds of services that we run," said the AWS spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The spokesperson added that after these events, the company implemented numerous additional safeguards, including mandatory peer review for production access. "Kiro puts developers in control-users need to configure which actions Kiro can take, and by default, Kiro requests authorization before taking any action," said the spokesperson. 

In mid-December, the Amazon cloud unit, AWS, suffered a 13-hour interruption to a system used by customers when engineers allowed its Kiro AI coding tool to carry out certain changes, the Financial Times reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The report noted that the agentic tool, which is capable of taking autonomous actions for users, decided to "delete and recreate the environment."

In October 2025, AWS experienced a major outage that disrupted several websites, apps, and online games.
